name: Electroweak sphaleron B+L violation
summary: The Standard Model SU(2)_L weak gauge sector has an Adler-Bell-Jackiw anomaly: the baryon-plus-lepton current B+L is not conserved (while B-L is). At zero temperature this violation is exponentially suppressed (rate ~ exp(-16 pi^2 / g^2) ~ 10^-165). Above the electroweak phase transition (T > 100 GeV) the sphaleron - the energy-9 TeV saddle-point between adjacent gauge vacua identified by Klinkhamer and Manton (1984) - is freely traversable and B+L is in thermal equilibrium. Sphalerons play a central role in all electroweak baryogenesis scenarios and in converting any primordial B-L asymmetry into the observed baryon asymmetry through leptogenesis.

addresses_puzzles.note: Any mechanism producing the baryon asymmetry must either (a) occur at T < T_EW and bypass sphalerons (e.g., Affleck-Dine), or (b) produce B-L at T > T_EW which sphalerons convert to a baryon excess in the ratio B = (28/79) * (B-L) (Khlebnikov-Shaposhnikov 1988). Sphaleron rate Gamma_sph ~ 25 alpha_W^5 T^4 in the symmetric phase decouples at T_EW; freeze-out condition selects which scenarios work.

touches_benchmarks.note: Sphaleron B+L violation does not mediate p -> e+ pi^0 at low energy; that requires GUT-scale processes. SM-only proton lifetime > 10^150 yr from sphaleron tunneling at T=0.

introduces.note: No new parameters - sphalerons are an exact SM consequence of the weak SU(2) anomaly. Computed entirely from SM input (g_W, M_W, m_h).

history: B+L anomaly: 't Hooft (1976) PRL 37, 8 - 'Symmetry breaking through Bell-Jackiw anomalies'. Sphaleron solution: Klinkhamer and Manton (1984) Phys. Rev. D 30, 2212. Thermal rate computation: Kuzmin-Rubakov-Shaposhnikov (1985) PLB 155, 36. Modern lattice rate: Moore (2000) hep-ph/0001274.

references.citation: Klinkhamer and Manton, 'A saddle point solution in the Weinberg-Salam theory', Phys. Rev. D 30, 2212 (1984)
't Hooft, 'Symmetry breaking through Bell-Jackiw anomalies', Phys. Rev. Lett. 37, 8 (1976)
Kuzmin, Rubakov, Shaposhnikov, 'On anomalous electroweak baryon-number non-conservation in the early universe', Phys. Lett. B 155, 36 (1985)
